sections.paragraph:
constr. עֲטֶרֶת, plur. עֲטָרֹות f.-

(1) a crown.-(a) convivial, Isa 28:1 (b) royal, a diadem, 2Sa 12:30 Psa 21:4 Son 3:11 Eze 21:31. Whatever is an ornament, or dignity, to any one, is figuratively designated a crown; Job 19:9, “he hath pulled down the crown from my head;” Pro 12:4, “a virtuous woman is a crown to her husband,” Pro 14:24 16:31 17:6 .

(2) [Atarah], pr.n. f. 1Ch 2:26.
